title: 1.1: Tests that draw() wraps text to next line with is_continued() flag set to True when DECAWM mode is enabled and text exceeds screen width

1.2: Tests that draw() truncates text without wrapping when text exceeds screen width given DECAWM mode is disabled, with cursor.x staying at last column position (`cursor.x = cols`) and `cursor.y` remaining at 0.
1.3: Tests that draw() shifts existing characters rightward when new text is drawn given IRM insert mode is enabled.
1.4: Tests that draw() renders wide Unicode characters with line().width(idx) returning 2 for wide characters and cursor advancing by sum of all character widths
1.5: Tests that draw() renders combining diacritical marks without consuming additional cells or advancing cursor position

1.6: Tests that draw() distributes wide Unicode characters across lines 0,1,2 maintaining complete character sequences without splitting individual wide characters when total width exceeds screen width
1.7: Tests that draw() processes repeat escape sequences to duplicate the last character multiple times at consecutive positions.
1.8: Tests that draw() duplicates last character N times when parse_bytes receives b'x1b[Nb' where N is the repeat count parameter (1 for b'x1b[b', 3 for b'x1b[3b')
1.9: Tests that the repeat escape sequence appends N characters to the line advancing cursor.x by the total width of the repeated characters.
1.10: Tests that draw() renders emoji sequences with skin tone modifiers as single combined glyphs occupying two-cell width.
1.11: Tests that draw() renders emoji with skin tone modifiers with str(s.line(0)) returning the exact combined codepoint sequence (base + modifier).
1.13: Tests that draw() renders regional indicator pairs that merge into flag glyphs occupying two-cell width
1.14: Tests that draw() renders partial regional indicator sequences as individual characters when separated by other text without forming complete flag pairs
1.15: Tests that draw() advances cursor by 2 positions after rendering merged regional indicator flag pairs
2.1: Places the test validating mode switching in kitty_tests/screen.py
2.2: Places the test validating wide Unicode rendering in kitty_tests/screen.py
2.3: Places the test validating repeat sequences in kitty_tests/screen.py
2.4: Places the test validating emoji modifiers in kitty_tests/screen.py
2.5: Places the test validating regional indicators in kitty_tests/screen.py
3.1: All tests use self.ae() assertion method inherited from BaseTest class for assertion.
3.2: All tests follow the test naming pattern test_<functionality><scenario> using snake_case.
4.2: Follows pattern of using parse_bytes(s, b'x1b[Nb') escape sequence format where N is optional numeric parameter to trigger character repetition for test verifying repeat sequences
